name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Install from the given requirements file. This option can be used multiple times

name: -c
--constraint
options.description: Constrain versions using the given constraints file. This option can be used multiple times

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Install from the given requirements file. This option can be used multiple times

options.name: --no-deps
options.description: Don’t install package dependencies

options.name: --global-option
options.description: Extra global options to be supplied to the setup.py call before the install command

options.name: --no-binary
options.description: Constrain versions using the given constraints file. This option can be used multiple times

options.name: --only-binary
options.description: Do not use source packages

options.name: --prefer-binary
options.description: Prefer older binary packages over newer source packages

options.name: --src
options.description: Directory to check out editable projects into

options.name: --pre
options.description: Include pre-release and development versions. By default, pip only finds stable versions

options.name: --require-hashes
options.description: Require a hash to check each requirement against, for repeatable installs

options.name: --progress-bar
options.description: Specify type of progress to be displayed

options.name: --no-build-isolation
options.description: Disable isolation when building a modern source distribution

options.name: --use-pep517
options.description: Use PEP 517 for building source distributions

options.name: --ignore-requires-python
options.description: Ignore the Requires-Python information

name: -d
--dest
options.description: Require a hash to check each requirement against, for repeatable installs

options.name: --platform
options.description: Only use wheels compatible with platform

options.name: --python-version
options.description: The Python interpreter version to use for wheel and “Requires-Python” compatibility checks

options.name: --implementation
options.description: Only use wheels compatible with Python implementation

options.name: --abi
options.description: Only use wheels compatible with Python abi <abi>, e.g. ‘pypy_41’

options.name: --no-clean
options.description: Don’t clean up build directories

name: -i
--index-url
options.description: Base URL of the Python Package Index

options.name: --no-index
options.description: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead)

options.name: --extra-index-url
options.description: Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to --index-url. Should follow the same rules as --index-url

name: -f
--find-links
options.description: Look for archives in the directory listing

subcommands.name: freeze
subcommands.description: Output installed packages in requirements format

name: -r
--requirement
options.description: Use the order in the given requirements file and its comments when generating output

name: -l
--local
options.description: If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not output globally-installed packages

options.name: --user
options.description: Only output packages installed in user-site

options.name: --path
options.description: Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --all
options.description: Do not skip these packages in the output: setuptools, distribute, pip, wheel

options.name: --exclude-editable
options.description: Exclude editable package from output

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude specified package from the output

name: -o
--outdated
options.description: List outdated packages

name: -u
--uptodate
options.description: List uptodate packages

name: -e
--editable
options.description: List editable projects

name: -l
--local
options.description: If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not list globally-installed packages

options.name: --user
options.description: Only output packages installed in user-site

options.name: --path
options.description: Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages (can be used multiple times)

options.name: --pre
options.description: Include pre-release and development versions. By default, pip only finds stable versions

options.name: --format
options.description: Select the output format among: columns (default), freeze, or json

options.name: --not-required
options.description: List packages that are not dependencies of installed packages

options.name: --exclude-editable
options.description: Exclude editable package from output

options.name: --include-editable
options.description: Include editable package from output

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Exclude specified package from the output

name: -i
--index-url
options.description: Base URL of the Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/simple)

options.name: --extra-index-url
options.description: Include pre-release and development versions. By default, pip only finds stable versions

options.name: --no-index
options.description: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead)

name: -f
--find-links
options.description: If a URL or path to an html file, then parse for links to archives such as sdist (.tar.gz) or wheel (.whl) files

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Verify installed packages have compatible dependencies

options.name: --editor
options.description: Editor to use to edit the file. Uses VISUAL or EDITOR environment variables if not provided

options.name: --global
options.description: Use the system-wide configuration file only

options.name: --user
options.description: Use the user configuration file only

options.name: --site
options.description: Use the current environment configuration file only

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: Inspect and manage pip's wheel cache

name: -a
--algorithm
options.description: The hash algorithm to use

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: A helper command used for command completion

options.description: Only use wheels compatible with platform
